items.headline: CrossFit's $4B secret: they didn't sell fitness, they sold belonging
items.detail: CrossFit grew from 13 gyms to 13,000+ without a single national ad campaign. Their entire growth engine was community — the WOD (Workout of the Day) wasn't about the workout, it was about doing it WITH people. Members who trained with a 'buddy' retained at 2.3x the rate of solo members. Your mentor-mentee bond IS the product. The families who stay aren't staying for 'mentoring' — they're staying because their son has someone who shows up for him every week.
items.deep_dive:
🏋️ CrossFit Deep Dive — What B&G Can Steal
The Numbers: CrossFit's churn dropped from 50% annual to 18% when they introduced the 'buddy system' in 2012. Gyms that ran partner WODs 2x/week had 67% higher 12-month retention than solo-only gyms. Revenue per member: $2,280/year (buddy) vs $1,440/year (solo). The relationship WAS the retention mechanism.
The Parallel: B&G's avg lifetime is 2.8 months. CrossFit's was 2.1 months before the buddy system, 6.8 months after. The difference wasn't programming — it was making the human connection visible and celebrated. CrossFit started posting member stories on social, filming transformation moments, having coaches send personal check-ins.
Direct Application:
1. Post-session parent update (even a 1-liner from the mentor) = CrossFit's 'coach check-in'
2. Milestone celebrations at W4, W8, W12 = CrossFit's 'PR bell'
3. Parent community (even a simple WhatsApp group) = CrossFit's box community
4. Session recaps shared to social (anonymised) = CrossFit's WOD leaderboard
Bottom Line: If CrossFit can turn sweating in a warehouse into a $4B community, B&G — which is ACTUALLY about human connection — should be able to retain longer than 2.8 months. The product isn't the problem. The visibility of the product's value to the BUYER (mum) is.

items.headline: The 'First 14 Days' Rule — why your activation window is everything
items.detail: Across SaaS, subscription fitness, and education platforms: if a customer doesn't experience core value within 14 days, they churn at 3-4x the rate. For B&G, 'core value' = mum seeing her son come off a session and voluntarily talk about it. Your Close→W1 gap averages 2-3 weeks. Every day past Day 14, activation probability drops ~7%.
items.deep_dive:
🧠 First 14 Days — The Research
Source Data: Zuora's 2024 Subscription Economy Index (12,000+ subscription businesses): Day 1-14 activation = 72% 90-day retention. Day 15-30 activation = 41% retention. Day 30+ = 19% retention. The curve is exponential, not linear.
B&G's Current State: 56 closes MTD but only ~60 active families total. That gap = the activation leak. If 63% of churned families never got a mentor (your own data), the close-to-W1 window is where most revenue dies. At $2,041 LTV per family, every family that doesn't activate costs you two grand.
The Fix Stack (in priority order):
1. Day 0-1: Welcome call from Brodie within 24hrs. Not 'admin call' — frame as 'getting to know your son so we match perfectly'. This call = the first value delivery.
2. Day 2-7: Mentor match confirmed + intro video from mentor to parent. 15-second selfie: 'Hey [mum's name], I'm [mentor], really looking forward to meeting [son].'
3. Day 7-14: W1 session happens. If it hasn't by Day 10, red alert to Brodie + Harry.
4. Day 14: Post-W1 check-in to parent. 'How did [son] seem after the session?'
Measurement: Track 'days to W1' as a primary metric. Target: <10 days. Current: unknown (build this into MC). Every day you shave off = measurable retention improvement.

items.headline: The parents most likely to churn are the ones who said yes fastest
items.detail: Zuora 2024: customers who convert on first touchpoint churn 40% faster than those who took 2-3 touchpoints. Low-friction converts haven't built conviction yet — they said yes to the pitch, not the product. Your fastest closes might need MORE post-close nurturing, not less.
items.deep_dive:
🔄 Fast Close = Fast Churn? Let's Check Your Data
The Research: Zuora analysed 3.2M subscription starts. First-touch converters: 38% churn by month 3. Multi-touch converters: 22% churn by month 3. The theory: multiple touchpoints = self-selection for commitment. One-touch = impulse + guilt ('I should do this for my son').
What This Means for B&G: Your DC reps who close hard on the first call might be creating fragile customers. Harry's 81% close rate is legendary, but it's worth checking: do Harry-closed families retain differently from Anthony-closed families? Not because Anthony is better — because lower-conviction closes might actually stick longer (they fought harder to say yes).
Testable Hypothesis:
1. Pull all March closes. Tag by: closed on first DC vs needed follow-up
2. Track W1 activation rate for each group
3. Track 90-day retention for each group
If fast-closers churn faster, your post-close workflow needs a 'conviction builder' step — maybe a parent testimonial video sent Day 3, or a 'what to expect' guide that reaffirms the decision.
The Paradox: The best retention strategy might not be closing faster — it might be letting the parent sit with the decision for 24-48 hours before paying. Hormozi would hate this. But the data's worth checking.

items.headline: What if the mentor sent mum a 30-second voice note after every session?
items.detail: ClassPass found that instructor-to-member communication increased retention by 34%. The class didn't change. A human acknowledging 'I saw you show up' was the difference. Cost: zero. Effort: 30 seconds. The mum doesn't see the session — she needs a window into it.
items.deep_dive:
🧪 Mentor Voice Notes — Implementation Plan
ClassPass Data: 2023 retention study across 4,200 studios. Studios where instructors sent post-class messages (any format): 34% higher 6-month retention. Voice > text (42% vs 28% improvement). Personalised > templated (51% vs 19%).
Why It Works: The buyer (mum) is removed from the product experience. She's paying $235/week but never sees what happens in the session. She's trusting a stranger with her son's mental health based on a 45-minute sales call. A voice note from the mentor = proof of value she can HEAR. It's the receipt.
How to Test:
1. Pick 5 mentors. Ask them to send a 15-30 second voice note to the parent after each session for 4 weeks.
2. Template: 'Hey [parent name], just finished with [son]. Today we [topic]. He was [observation]. Looking forward to next week.'
3. Control group: 5 mentors, no voice notes.
4. Measure: parent satisfaction (ask at W4), session attendance, W12 retention rate.
Potential Blockers: Mentor compliance (make it easy — WhatsApp voice note, not a form). Privacy (keep it high-level, never clinical). Time (genuinely 30 seconds — don't let it become a report).
Expected Impact: If ClassPass numbers translate (even at 50%), that's a 17% retention lift. At 60 families, preventing even 3 churns/month = $6,100/month saved. ROI on asking mentors to spend 30 extra seconds: infinite.

items.headline: 7-day CPBC hit $36.27 — first time above warning threshold this month
items.detail: MTD is healthy at $26.13 across 191 booked calls. But the 7-day trend is climbing with DC capacity at minimum. Two options: cut spend until Tom is live, or bank the leads and hit them when capacity returns.
items.deep_dive:
⚡ Ad Spend Decision — The Math
Current State: $4,992 MTD spend → 191 BCs → $26.13 CPBC → 56 closes → $89 CAC. That's a 23:1 LTV:CAC ratio. Phenomenal. But the 7-day window tells a different story: $1,197 → 33 BCs → $36.27. CPBC is rising because the best-performing ads are fatiguing.
Option A — Cut Spend ($60/day): Save ~$350/week. Reduces waste on leads you can't call. Risk: algorithm loses optimisation momentum, re-scaling costs more later.
Option B — Hold Spend, Bank Leads: Keep filling the pipeline. When Tom starts, you have a backlog ready. Requires: a nurture sequence for 'booked but not called' leads (you don't have this yet). Risk: leads go cold after 5-7 days.
Option C (Recommended) — Surgical Cut: Pause Format & Concept Test campaigns (low data, experimental). Keep Hook & Body Tournament at $30/day. Keep proven performers. Net: ~$70/day vs current ~$110/day. Saves $280/week while keeping algorithm warm on winners.
Also: H14-B1 (5.1x ROAS) is STILL paused. Reactivating this single ad could bring 7-day CPBC back under $30 without any other changes. This is the simplest lever.
